name: Metrics & Time-Series Monitoring
def: Numeric measurements over time (counters, gauges, histograms) aggregated for monitoring and alerting.
problem: Track system health and performance trends cheaply at scale, and alert on anomalies.
mechanism: Instrument code to emit metrics; a time-series DB (Prometheus) scrapes/stores them; dashboards and alert rules evaluate aggregates and percentiles.
failures: Alerting on averages, missing tail latency
Unbounded label cardinality blowing up storage
myths: "Average latency is enough" — p99/p999 tails drive user pain; use histograms
